title: ceterach cordatum var. capense
intro: Ceterach cordatum var. capense, also known as the Cape fir club moss or a type of ceterach, is a species of plant in the lycopodiaceae family, characterized by its small size and fern-like appearance. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Ceterach cordatum var. capense are largely anecdotal, but may include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
